name: Beirão (Beira Portuguese)
notes: Beirão / Beira Portuguese (Beira Litoral: Coimbra, Aveiro; Beira Alta: Viseu, Guarda; Beira Baixa: Castelo Branco), a northern-central EP dialect. Cintra places the Beirões in the Baixo-Minhoto-Duriense-Beirão group. MODELLED FEATURES: (1) APICO-ALVEOLAR-ONLY SIBILANT SYSTEM — the 'famous' s beirão: ⟨s/ss⟩ AND ⟨c/ç/z⟩ all realise as apico-alveolar [s̺]/[z̺] (Cintra 1971 pp.88, 93 — the most palatalised apical variant is 'vulgarmente conhecida pelo nome de s beirão'; Álvarez Pérez 2014 pp.37-38, Beirões 'kept only the apico-alveolar branch'; Pérez notes the apical core is clearest in the districts of Viseu, Guarda and Castelo Branco). Coda ⟨s/z⟩ keep chiado [ʃ]/[ʒ]. (2) BETACISM — /v/–/b/ merged to /b/ ~ [β]; Pérez 2014 pp.35-37 documents an extensive bilabial-merger area in the eastern districts of Guarda and Castelo Branco that Cintra's original map omitted. A conservative alveolar rhotic (trill [r]) is enumerated as a variant of /ʁ/. (3) BEIRA-BAIXA STRESSED /u/ → [y] PALATALISATION — Cintra (1971, p.14 of the CVC reflow) delimits the Beira-Baixa / Alto-Alentejo zone — 'uma região que tem como principais núcleos urbanos Castelo-Branco e Portalegre' — by exactly the isogloss of stressed-/u/ palatalisation: 'Como isófona que possa marcar o limite da zona, parece-me preferível escolher, a da palatalização, em maior ou menor grau, da vogal tónica u', within 'uma profunda alteração de timbre de todo o sistema vocálico, principalmente do tónico'; he adds that at the zone's edges the u-timbre change is the trait that stays most perceptible ('o traço que permanece mais perceptível é a alteração do timbre do u'). The explicit front-rounded [y] IPA value is written in the adjacent Barlavento-Algarvio passage of the same 'reacção em cadeia' — 'a palatalização da lábio-velar [u] em [y]' — the two zones sharing the /u/-palatalisation isogloss. Encoded as the allophone rule BEI_U_FRONTING (stressed nucleus only), mirroring the sister mainland zone pt-PT-x-alentejo (ALE_U_FRONTING) and pt-PT-x-algarve: because this is a whole-tonic-system MAINLAND chain shift (not the insular São Miguel open-nucleus process), it fronts even before a tautosyllabic coda liquid (sul → [ˈs̺yɫ]) — deliberately WITHOUT the pt-PT-x-acores ACO_U_KEEP_BEFORE_CODA block, which is a Rogers-1948 São-Miguel-specific conditioning with no mainland source. A PROCLITIC GUARD (word_exceptions, same design as pt-PT-x-alentejo/pt-PT-x-algarve) pins the monosyllabic clitics o/os/no/nos/do/dos/ao/aos/pelo/pelos/um/uns to their inherited Beira nuclei so the word-level stress detector cannot mis-front do → [ˈdy] etc. SCOPE / INTRA-NODE LIMIT (documented, not faked): /u/-fronting is the Beira-BAIXA (Castelo-Branco) sub-zone realisation; Beira Litoral (Coimbra, Aveiro) and Beira Alta (Viseu, Guarda) sit in the Baixo-Minhoto-Duriense-Beirão group and do NOT palatalise tonic /u/ — this general Beira node models the strongest (Beira-Baixa) realisation, the same modelling stance as pt-PT-x-acores (Terceira gold vs the São Miguel /u/-fronting). The wider chain shift ([a] velarisation, [e]/[ɛ] labialisation, [ɔ]/[o] raising) is left to notes to avoid collapsing the vowel space; only the categorical /u/ → [y] is realised. Inherits pt-PT base.

sources.author: Vasconcelos, J.L. de
sources.title: Esquisse d'une dialectologie portugaise
sources.notes: Classical dialectological survey; Beira section foundational

sources.author: Cintra, L. F. Lindley
sources.title: Nova proposta de classificação dos dialectos galego-portugueses
sources.notes: Foundational EP dialect classification. p.93: the Baixo-Minhoto-Duriense-Beirao group reduces the archaic four-sibilant system to two apico-alveolar sibilants [s̺ z̺]; p.88: /v/-/b/ merger and apico-alveolar sibilants are diagnostic northern features. p.14 (CVC reflow): delimits the Beira-Baixa / Alto-Alentejo zone (núcleos Castelo-Branco e Portalegre) by the isogloss of stressed-/u/ palatalisation — 'Como isófona que possa marcar o limite da zona, parece-me preferível escolher, a da palatalização, em maior ou menor grau, da vogal tónica u' — within 'uma profunda alteração de timbre de todo o sistema vocálico, principalmente do tónico', noting the u-timbre change is the trait most perceptible at the zone's edges; the explicit [y] value ('a palatalização da lábio-velar [u] em [y]') is written in the adjacent Barlavento-Algarvio passage of the same 'reacção em cadeia'. Grounds BEI_U_FRONTING for the Beira-Baixa (Castelo Branco) sub-zone.

sources.author: Álvarez Pérez, Xosé Afonso
sources.title: European Portuguese dialectal features: a comparison with Cintra's proposal
sources.notes: ALEPG-corpus quantitative reassessment. pp.37-38: Baixo-Minhotos, Durienses and Beirões 'kept only the apico-alveolar branch' (both s/ss and c/ç/z merge to apical), vs the conservative four-sibilant Transmontano/Alto-Minhoto. pp.35-37: /b/-/v/ merger extends along the coast beyond Coimbra and through the eastern districts (Guarda, Castelo Branco).

sources.author: Brissos, Fernando
sources.title: New insights into Portuguese central-southern dialects: understanding their present and past forms through acoustic data from stressed vowels
sources.notes: Acoustic study of central-southern EP stressed vowels (the zone Cintra delimits by the stressed-/u/ isogloss, spanning Beira-Baixa to the Algarve). Documents stressed /u/ → [y] ([tˈyd] 'tudo', [lˈymɨ] 'lume') and cites Lüdtke (1957) and Maia (1975–78) that the palatalisation is stressed-only — corroborating acoustic source for the Beira-Baixa BEI_U_FRONTING modelled from Cintra's p.14 zone-defining isogloss.

stress.notes: Acordo Ortográfico 1990 conventions: paroxytone default; written accents override; final -i/-u/-r/-l/-z/-x/-im/-om/-um/-ns and nasal diphthongs attract final stress. Unmarked -em/-am stay paroxytone (homem, falam).

allophone_rules.notes: Palatalisation/fronting of the stressed labio-velar /u/ to the front rounded [y] — the feature by which Cintra (1971, p.14 of the CVC reflow) delimits the Beira-Baixa / Alto-Alentejo zone (núcleos Castelo-Branco e Portalegre): 'a palatalização, em maior ou menor grau, da vogal tónica u', part of 'uma profunda alteração de timbre de todo o sistema vocálico, principalmente do tónico'. The explicit [y] value ('a palatalização da lábio-velar [u] em [y]') is written in the adjacent Barlavento-Algarvio passage of the same 'reacção em cadeia'; Brissos (2014) documents it acoustically for the central-southern zone ([tˈyd] tudo, [lˈymɨ] lume). Restricted to the STRESSED nucleus (stress='stressed'): unstressed /u/ is untouched, so it does not feed on the inherited pt-PT unstressed /o/→[u] reduction. Being a whole-tonic-system MAINLAND chain shift, it fronts even before a tautosyllabic coda liquid (sul → [ˈs̺yɫ]), mirroring pt-PT-x-alentejo/pt-PT-x-algarve and deliberately WITHOUT the insular pt-PT-x-acores ACO_U_KEEP_BEFORE_CODA block (a Rogers-1948 São-Miguel open-nucleus conditioning with no mainland source). NOTES-ONLY (not modelled): the wider chain shift ([a] velarisation, [e]/[ɛ] labialisation, [ɔ]/[o] raising) is left to notes to avoid collapsing the vowel space. SCOPE: the Beira-BAIXA (Castelo-Branco) sub-zone realisation — Beira Litoral/Alta do not palatalise tonic /u/; this general Beira node models the strongest realisation (same stance as pt-PT-x-acores). A proclitic guard (see word_exceptions) keeps monosyllabic clitics — o/os/no/nos/do/dos/ao/aos/pelo/pelos/um/uns — on their inherited [u] nucleus, since the word-level stress detector mis-marks them as stressed.

location.notes: Viseu — a representative locality of the Beiras (Beira Litoral, Alta and Baixa), an area far larger than any one town. [Wikidata Q117676 P625]
